verses.latin_vulgate: Alleluja.   In exitu Israël de Ægypto,  domus Jacob de populo barbaro,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּצֵ֣את יִ֭שְׂרָאֵל מִמִּצְרָ֑יִם בֵּ֥ית יַ֝עֲקֹ֗ב מֵעַ֥ם לֹעֵֽז׃
verses.king_james: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language;

verses.latin_vulgate: facta est Judæa sanctificatio ejus ;  Israël potestas ejus.
verses.king_james: Judah was his sanctuary, and Israel his dominion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mare vidit, et fugit ;  Jordanis conversus est retrorsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַיָּ֣ם רָ֭אָה וַיָּנֹ֑ס הַ֝יַּרְדֵּ֗ן יִסֹּ֥ב לְאָחֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: The sea saw it, and fled: Jordan was driven back.

verses.latin_vulgate: Montes exsultaverunt ut arietes,  et colles sicut agni ovium.
verses.king_james: The mountains skipped like rams, and the little hills like lambs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est tibi, mare, quod fugisti ?  et tu, Jordanis, quia conversus es retrorsum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־לְּךָ֣ הַ֭יָּם כִּ֣י תָנ֑וּס הַ֝יַּרְדֵּ֗ן תִּסֹּ֥ב לְאָחֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?

verses.latin_vulgate: montes, exsultastis sicut arietes ?  et colles, sicut agni ovium ?
verses.king_james: Ye mountains, that ye skipped like rams; and ye little hills, like lambs?

verses.latin_vulgate: A facie Domini mota est terra,  a facie Dei Jacob :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִלִּפְנֵ֣י אָ֭דוֹן ח֣וּלִי אָ֑רֶץ מִ֝לִּפְנֵ֗י אֱל֣וֹהַּ יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.king_james: Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui convertit petram in stagna aquarum,  et rupem in fontes aquarum.
verses.king_james: Which turned the rock into a standing water, the flint into a fountain of waters.
